resources.string:
Hacker Tracker is a conference companion app. This policy describes how the Android app handles data.

Optional usage statistics
Anonymous usage statistics are off by default. If you enable “Send anonymous usage statistics” in Settings, the app may send interaction events (for example screen views and conference selection) to Google Firebase Analytics.

Optional crash reports
Crash reports are off by default. If you enable “Send crash reports” in Settings, the app may send crash and diagnostic logs to Google Firebase Crashlytics.

Anonymous account
The app signs in anonymously with Firebase Authentication to maintain a conference session. This creates a random user ID. It is not linked to your name, email, or phone number.

Conference content and messaging
Schedule and related content are loaded from Google Firebase (Firestore and Remote Config). Push notifications use Firebase Cloud Messaging when you allow notifications.

Location
On Android 9 (API 28) and below, the app may request precise location only to configure conference Wi‑Fi. Location is not used for tracking or advertising. On Android 10 and newer, Wi‑Fi join does not require location permission.

Sharing and sale of data
We do not sell your data. Google/Firebase act as service providers that process data needed to run Analytics (if enabled), Crashlytics (if enabled), Authentication, Firestore, Remote Config, and Cloud Messaging.

Your choices
You can turn usage statistics and crash reports on or off at any time in Settings. Clearing app data or uninstalling the app removes local preferences and ends the local anonymous session.
